classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
And Indeed that Christ Was Not Only Man, But God Also; That Even as He Was the Son of Man, So Also He Was the Son of God.

ccel.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

title: And Indeed that Christ Was Not Only Man, But God Also; That Even as He Was the Son of Man, So Also He Was the Son of God.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But lest, from the fact of asserting that our Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God, the Creator, was manifested in the substance of the true body, we should seem either to have given assent to other heretics, who in this place maintain that He is man only and alone, and therefore desire to prove that He was a man bare and solitary; and lest we should seem to have afforded them any ground for objecting, we do not so express doctrine concerning the substance of His body, as to say that He is only and alone man, but so as to maintain, by the association of the divinity of the Word in that very materiality, that He was also God according to the Scriptures.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For there is a great risk of saying that the Saviour of the human race was only man; that the Lord of all, and the Chief of the world, to whom all things were delivered, and all things were granted by His Father, by whom all things were ordained, all things were created, all things were arranged, the King of all ages and times, the Prince of all the angels, before whom there is none but the Father, was only man, and denying to Him divine authority in these things.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For this contempt of the heretics will recoil also upon God the Father, if God the Father could not beget God the Son.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But, moreover, no blindness of the heretics shall prescribe to the truth.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Nor, because they maintain one thing in Christ and, do not maintain another, they see one side of Christ and do not see another, shall there be taken away from us that which they do not see for the sake of that which they do.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For they regard the weaknesses in Him as if they were a man’s weaknesses, but they do not count the powers as if they were a God’s powers.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: They keep in mind the infirmities of the flesh, they exclude the powers of the divinity; when if this argument from the infirmities of Christ is of avail to the result of proving Him to be man from His infirmities, the argument of divinity in Him gathered from His powers avails to the result also of asserting Him to be God from His works.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For if His sufferings show in Him human frailty, why may not His works assert in Him divine power?

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For if this should not avail to assert Him to be God from His powers, neither can His sufferings avail to show Him to be man also from them.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For whatever principle be adopted on one or the other side, will be found to be maintained.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For there will be a risk that He should not be shown to be man from His sufferings, if He could not also be approved as God by His powers.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: We must not then lean to one side and evade the other side, because any one who should exclude one portion of the truth will never hold the perfect truth.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For Scripture as much announces Christ as also God, as it announces God Himself as man.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: It has as much described Jesus Christ to be man, as moreover it has also described Christ the Lord to be God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Because it does not set forth Him to be the Son of God only, but also the Son of man; nor does it only say, the Son of man, but it has also been accustomed to speak of Him as the Son of God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: So that being of both, He is both, lest if He should be one only, He could not be the other.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For as nature itself has prescribed that he must be believed to be a man who is of man, so the same nature prescribes also that He must be believed to be God who is of God; but if he should not also be God when he is of God, no more should he be man although he should be of man.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And thus both doctrines would be endangered in one and the other way, by one being convicted to have lost belief in the other.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Let them, therefore, who read that Jesus Christ the Son of man is man, read also that this same Jesus is called also God and the Son of God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For in the manner that as man He is of Abraham, so also as God He is before Abraham himself.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He is as man the “Son of David,” so as God He is proclaimed David’s Lord.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He was made as man “under the law,” so as God He is declared to be “Lord of the Sabbath.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He suffers, as man, the condemnation, so as God He is found to have all judgment of the quick and dead.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He is born as man subsequent to the world, so as God He is manifested to have been before the world.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same way as He was begotten as man of the seed of David, so also the world is said to have been ordained by Him as God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same way as He was as man after many, so as God He was before all.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He was as man inferior to others, so as God He was greater than all.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He ascended as man into heaven, so as God He had first descended thence.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the same manner as He goes as man to the Father, so as the Son in obedience to the Father He shall descend thence.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: So if imperfections in Him prove human frailty, majesties in Him affirm divine power.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For the risk is, in reading of both, to believe not both, but one of the two.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Wherefore as both are read of in Christ, let both be believed; that so finally the faith may be true, being also complete.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For if of two principles one gives way in the faith, and the other, and that indeed which is of least importance, be taken up for belief, the rule of truth is thrown into confusion; and that boldness will not confer salvation, but instead of salvation will effect a great risk of death from the overthrow of the faith.
